title: Information System Activity Review — Cross-Source ePHI Access Summary

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(D) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(D) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.3: Information System Activity Review — Cross-Source ePHI Access Summary.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.3' running on index=epic sourcetype=epic:audit, index=citrix sourcetype=citrix:session, index=vpn sourcetype=paloalto:globalprotect, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
(index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart") OR (index=cerner sourcetype="cerner:audit" Action="PatientChartView") OR (index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" Application="*Hyperspace*") OR (index=vpn sourcetype="paloalto:globalprotect" status="success")
| eval user=coalesce(USER_ID, PractitionerId, user, src_user)
| eval PAT_ID=coalesce(PAT_ID, PatientId)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients_touched dc(host) as systems by _time, user
| sort - patients_touched
description: Produces a daily leadership-ready rollup of who touched ePHI across EHR, VPN, and databases so workforce activity review obligations are operationally met, not ad hoc.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cerner, citrix, epic and sourcetypes cerner:audit, citrix:session, citrix:session`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align user ID formats via `identity_lookup.csv`; (2) restrict index ACLs to privacy/security; (3) schedule daily PDF/dashboard to HIPAA committee; (4) retain per legal record retention policy. Indexes required: `index=cerner`, `index=citrix`, `index=epic`, `index=vpn`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cerner:audit`, `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `sourcetype=citrix:session``, `sourcetype=epic:audit`.
visualization: Heatmap (users x day), Table (top reviewers), Single value (distinct users accessing charts).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user span=1d | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `index=vpn` `sourcetype=paloalto:globalprotect`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align user ID formats via `identity_lookup.csv`; (2) restrict index ACLs to privacy/security; (3) schedule daily PDF/dashboard to HIPAA committee; (4) retain per legal record retention policy.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart") OR (index=cerner sourcetype="cerner:audit" Action="PatientChartView") OR (index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" Application="*Hyperspace*") OR (index=vpn sourcetype="paloalto:globalprotect" status="success")
| eval user=coalesce(USER_ID, PractitionerId, user, src_user)
| eval PAT_ID=coalesce(PAT_ID, PatientId)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients_touched dc(host) as systems by _time, user
| sort - patients_touched
```

Understanding this SPL

**Information System Activity Review — Cross-Source ePHI Access Summary (§164.308(a)(1)(ii)(D))** — Produces a daily leadership-ready rollup of who touched ePHI across EHR, VPN, and databases so workforce activity review obligations are operationally met, not ad hoc.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `index=vpn` `sourcetype=paloalto:globalprotect`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: epic, cerner, citrix, vpn; **sourcetype**: epic:audit, cerner:audit, citrix:session, paloalto:globalprotect. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=epic, index=cerner, index=citrix, index=vpn…. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **user** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **PAT_ID** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user span=1d | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Information System Activity Review — Cross-Source ePHI Access Summary (§164.308(a)(1)(ii)(D))** — Produces a daily leadership-ready rollup of who touched ePHI across EHR, VPN, and databases so workforce activity review obligations are operationally met, not ad hoc.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `index=vpn` `sourcetype=paloalto:globalprotect`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (users x day), Table (top reviewers), Single value (distinct users accessing charts).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for produces a daily leadership-ready rollup of who touched ephi across ehr, vpn, and databases so workforce activity review obligations are operationally met, not ad hoc. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
